title: Reading Files 📖
description: Open and read text files in Python.

slides.title: File I/O — Talk to Files! 📂
slides.content:
# Reading Files

So far, all your data disappears when the program ends. **Files** let you save and load data permanently!

Python's built-in `open()` function opens files:

```
file = open("data.txt", "r")  # "r" = read mode
content = file.read()
print(content)
file.close()  # Always close!
```

**File modes:**
| Mode | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `"r"` | Read (default) |
| `"w"` | Write (overwrites!) |
| `"a"` | Append (add to end) |
| `"x"` | Create (error if exists) |

slides.title: Reading Methods 📋
slides.content:
## Three Ways to Read

```
# Read entire file as one string
content = file.read()

# Read one line at a time
line = file.readline()

# Read all lines as a list
lines = file.readlines()
```

**Most common pattern** — loop line by line:
```
for line in file:
    print(line.strip())  # strip() removes \n
```

This is memory-efficient because it reads one line at a time instead of loading the entire file!

slides.title: Reading Demo 🎬
slides.content: Different ways to read file content:
slides.codeSnippet:
# Create a file to read
with open("demo.txt", "w") as f:
    f.write("Line 1: Hello\nLine 2: World\nLine 3: Python")

# Read entire file
with open("demo.txt", "r") as f:
    content = f.read()
    print("Full content:")
    print(content)

print("\n--- Line by line ---")
# Read line by line
with open("demo.txt", "r") as f:
    for line in f:
        print(f"  > {line.strip()}")

quizData.question: What mode opens a file for reading?
quizData.explanation: "r" is the read mode. It's also the default if no mode is specified.

quizData.question: What does readlines() return?
quizData.explanation: readlines() returns a list where each element is a line from the file.
